explanation_box.text: Perhaps this reptile had a low fertility score due to its orange color as this color would easily be seen by predators that would eat the reptile.

This score is from a different season, one that allowed the fertility score to rise by one point. Even raising the score one point, this is still a low fertility score. Again, perhaps it's due to the bright orange color that can easily be seen by predators among the green foliage.
Perhaps it is due to the reptile's green color that more easily hides the reptile from predators.

Perhaps it's due to the reptile's green coloring that easily hides it among the green leaves and the higher fertility score (from a different season) reflects a season that had more rain, more greenery along the forest floor that made its green coloring an even better cover.
It has to be the color. This orange color is highly visible to predators that catch and kill the reptile and its offspring.
The reptile is able to hide among the leaves on the forest floor. This must have been a season of relatively high rain amounts that greened up the forest floor where these reptiles live.
Due to its green color that blends with the greenery in its home on the forest floor.
Due to its color, the reptile is highly visible to predators, yet during this season it was able to fend off predators and live to have a higher fertility score thann would be expected for its color.
Due to its bright orange color that advertises the reptile's presence to predators.
Due to its coloring that could hide (live among the green elements of the forest) from its predators.
Due to its bright orange color that makes it stand out in the greenery of the forest where it lives.
The reptile is green and it perfectly hides the reptile among the greenery of the forest floor.
This was a good year for this reptile that successfully hid from its predators due to its green color and successfully taught its offspring to do the same.
Due to its green coloring that allows it to hide from predators, but it was unable to teach all his offspring as it had successfully done in seasons past.
Due to its bright orange coloring.
Due to its bright orange coloring and moderate success at teaching its offspring how to hide.

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they were GREEN or RED. Did being GREEN or RED make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did color matter to the fertility of reptiles?

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they had ROUND feet or POINTY, CURLY feet. Did having CURLY/POINTY or ROUND feet make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did foot shape matter to the fertility of reptiles?
